item.title: Running PostgreSQL in memory with docker
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Introduction

InôÙteôÙracôÙting wiôÙth a daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse, can be a reôÙguôÙlar taôÙsk of a deôÙveôÙloôÙpeôÙr, and for thaôÙt we would like to enôÙsuôÙre that we are deôÙveôÙloôÙping and tesôÙting in siôÙtuaôÙtions cloôÙse ôÙto a real imôÙpleôÙmenôÙtaôÙtioôÙn; theôÙreôÙfoôÙre, using the saôÙme daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse as in ôÙproôÙducôÙtion can help deôÙtecôÙting isôÙsues earôÙlôÙy.

HoôÙweôÙveôÙr, seôÙtting up an enôÙtiôÙre daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse serôÙver for deôÙveôÙloôÙpôÙmenôÙt, can beôÙ ôÙcumôÙberôÙsoôÙme. HoôÙpeôÙfuôÙlly noôÙwaôÙdaôÙys, moôÙdern opeôÙraôÙting sysôÙteôÙms like LiôÙnux haôÙve ôÙgreat tools and feaôÙtuôÙres that we could take adôÙvanôÙtaôÙge of.

In parôÙtiôÙcuôÙlaôÙr, I would like to seôÙtup a simôÙple daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse loôÙcaôÙlly usinôÙg doôÙcker, and stoôÙring the daôÙta in meôÙmoôÙrôÙy.

The idea is simôÙple: run a doôÙcker conôÙtaiôÙner wiôÙth the imaôÙge for PosôÙtgresSôÙQL, uôÙsing a tmôÙpfs [1] [2] as stoôÙraôÙge for the daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse (a raôÙmôÙfs could alôÙsoôÙ ôÙbe usôÙeôÙd).

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 3 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Returning data in PostgreSQL
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
In orôÙder to get the best perôÙforôÙmanôÙce from our daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse enôÙgiôÙne, most of the ôÙtiôÙmeôÙs, we haôÙve to take adôÙvanôÙtaôÙge of its exôÙcluôÙsiôÙve feaôÙtuôÙres. This has the ôÙdiôÙsadôÙvanôÙtaôÙge of geôÙtting farôÙther away from stanôÙdarôÙd, ANôÙSI coôÙde, and in the caôÙse of RDBM enôÙgiôÙnes, this alôÙso meanôÙs, most of the ORôÙMs wiôÙll not woôÙrk.

BeôÙsiôÙdes thaôÙt, it could soôÙmeôÙtiôÙmes be worôÙth exôÙploôÙring what we can gain by aôÙdapôÙting to the enôÙgiôÙne.

This is soôÙmeôÙthing learnt from the feeôÙdbaôÙck of my taôÙlk giôÙven at EuôÙroôÙPôÙyôÙthon 2016. After the taôÙlk, an attenôÙdee told me ôÙthat he liked it, and asked me about the coôÙde porôÙtraôÙyed in one of the sliôÙdes, ôÙthe one about deôÙcoôÙraôÙtors wiôÙth the examôÙple of moôÙving soôÙme reôÙcorôÙds from one taôÙble ôÙto anoôÙtheôÙr. He asked me if I ran coôÙde like that one in proôÙducôÙtioôÙn, to whiôÙch I anôÙsweôÙred no: the coôÙde is enôÙtiôÙreôÙly ficôÙtioôÙnaôÙl, and it was doôÙne for the meôÙre ôÙpurôÙpoôÙses of the examôÙpleôÙs, so I just neeôÙded an exôÙcuôÙse for a taôÙsk that could beôÙ ôÙdoôÙne in two SQL staôÙteôÙmenôÙts, so the examôÙple wiôÙth the deôÙcoôÙraôÙtor can be shoôÙwn.

The coôÙde of the examôÙple foôÙlloôÙws:

INSERT INTO archive_orders
SELECT * from orders
WHERE order_date < '2016-01-01';

DELETE from orders WHERE order_date < '2016-01-01';

Then I learnt that theôÙre is anoôÙther way of wriôÙting that sort of staôÙteôÙmenôÙts, wiôÙth the "reôÙturôÙninôÙg" senôÙtenôÙce of PosôÙtgreSôÙQôÙL.

It would be re-wôÙriôÙtten like:

WITH deleted as (
    DELETE FROM orders WHERE order_date < '2016-01-01'
    RETURNING *
)
INSERT INTO archive_orders select * from deleted;

Or... the other way arounôÙd:

WITH to_delete as(
    INSERT INTO archive_orders
    SELECT id, description, order_date FROM orders WHERE order_date < '2016-01-01'
    RETURNING id
)
DELETE FROM orders where id in (select id from to_delete);

The inôÙteôÙresôÙting point heôÙre, is that it enôÙtails a sinôÙgle coôÙmôÙmanôÙd, insôÙtead of ôÙtwo. So this can be doôÙne wiôÙth a sinôÙgle caôÙll to the daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse, saving an exôÙtra ôÙround triôÙp.

The point heôÙre is that the deôÙleôÙte staôÙteôÙment of PosôÙtgreSôÙQL (as weôÙll as the ôÙrest of the staôÙteôÙmenôÙts, INôÙSERôÙT, UPôÙDAôÙTE, for insôÙtanôÙce), can be speôÙciôÙfied toôÙ ôÙreôÙturn the daôÙta they affecôÙt, and this can be usôÙed in an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiaôÙte taôÙble toôÙ ôÙpiôÙpe it to anoôÙther staôÙteôÙmenôÙt.

By deôÙfaulôÙt, if you run the deôÙleôÙte staôÙteôÙmenôÙt, it should reôÙturn the numôÙber of aôÙffecôÙted roôÙws, like:

delete from orders where id > 3;

DELETE 4

BuôÙt, we can make it to reôÙturn the roôÙws theôÙmselôÙveôÙs:

delete from orders where id > 3 returning \*;

id|   description    |     order_date
----+------------------+---------------------
4 | to be archived   | 2014-12-09 00:00:00
5 | First sale order | 2016-07-17 00:00:00
6 | First sale order | 2016-07-20 00:00:00
7 | First sale order | 2016-07-24 00:00:00
(4 rows)

DELETE 4

Or, speôÙciôÙfic coôÙlumns if we seôÙlect theôÙm:

delete from orders where id > 3 returning id, description;

id |   description
----+------------------
4 | to be archived
5 | First sale order
6 | First sale order
7 | First sale order
(4 rows)

DELETE 4

So, we can use the "reôÙturôÙninôÙg" feaôÙtuôÙre of PosôÙtgreSôÙQôÙL, to do in a sinôÙgle coôÙmôÙmanôÙd what we usuaôÙlly would do in two or moôÙre, and in soôÙme caôÙses, it miôÙght beôÙ ôÙsoôÙmeôÙthing worôÙth exôÙploôÙrinôÙg. It was great learôÙning things like this one, anôÙd ôÙgeôÙtting tips as a reôÙsult from the feeôÙdbaôÙck of the taôÙlk (it does not chanôÙge the ôÙmeaôÙninôÙg, and the examôÙple could reôÙmain the saôÙme for the afoôÙreôÙmenôÙtioôÙned reaôÙsonôÙs; it is just an examôÙple :-).
